channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Nobody teaches you how to think like a senior engineer
item.description: Nobody teaches you how to think like a senior engineer because the people who can teach it are too busy doing it. They review your PR and leave one comment that reframes the entire thing — "what...

item.title: Metadata Filtering Before Vector Search: The Recall Win Nobody Measures
item.description: Book: RAG Pocket Guide: Retrieval, Chunking, and Reranking Patterns for Production Also by me: Thinking in Go (2-book series) — Complete Guide to Go Programming + Hexagonal Architecture in Go My...

item.title: What Is Performance Testing? A Complete Guide for QA Teams
item.description: In 2018, a major ticketing platform crashed during a high-profile concert sale. Millions of users rushed to buy tickets at the same time, overwhelming the system. Customers were locked out,...

item.title: Fitz CLI builder: como typer, pero en el lenguaje
item.description: Construí herramientas CLI nativas en Fitz con @command, sin librería que instalar. Help auto-generado, flags con type coercion, positional args por convención, binario nativo a la salida. El mismo...

item.title: Fitz CLI builder: like typer, but in the language
item.description: Build native CLI tools in Fitz with @command, no library to install. Help auto-generated, type-coerced flags, positional args by convention, native binary out the door. The same language that...

item.title: The Paradox of Power: Why Anthropic Released and Then Restricted Claude Fable 5
item.description: On June 9, 2026, Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, which was described as the most capable AI model publicly available at the time. Within 72 hours, access was cut off globally after intervention...

item.title: When to Move Beyond LiteLLM (And When Not To)
item.description: LiteLLM is one of the most useful tools in the modern AI stack, and I want to say that clearly before anything else. If you're building an AI application and haven't tried it: a working proxy to...

item.title: Bun rewrote itself from Zig to Rust in 9 days with an LLM. That's terrifying.
item.description: It took nine days to rewrite all of Bun from Zig to Rust using an LLM and get the new code to the point where I could merge it. I saw that and my reaction was not “cool.” It was “oh no.” Let me be...

item.title: I Spent $200 in One Day on AI Tokens to Build 86 Browser Tools — A Solo Founder's Complete Build Log
item.description: 86 tools. Zero backend. One person. Two hospital visits. $200 in tokens in 24 hours. This is what building in public actually looks like — not the highlight reel, but the raw commit history of a...

item.title: Build a Faceless TikTok Channel Pipeline with FFmpeg Micro
item.description: Originally published at ffmpeg-micro.com Faceless TikTok channels post 3-5 times a day. No filming, no editing software, no face on camera. The best ones gross $2K-10K/month from the Creator Fund...

item.title: A German Court Has Ruled That Google Is Liable for False Statements Generated by AI Overviews
item.description: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.

item.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to...

item.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living
item.description: Andrew Yang made a list of everything Americans overpay for — housing, food, wireless — and thinks the next startup gold rush is giving that money back.

item.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
item.description: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people,"...

item.title: Anthropic Says It’s Taking Claude Fable 5 Offline to Comply With US Government Order
item.description: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.

item.title: Valve just imported 13 tons of VR headsets in one day
item.description: On June 10th, the German container ship Posen docked in Los Angeles after a two-week voyage from Shanghai. As Valve watcher Brad Lynch notes, it was almost certainly carrying the first mass...

item.title: Meta Employees Absolutely Hate Mark Zuckerberg’s Plan for a Companywide AI Hackathon
item.description: “I’m not sure that this company supports a hackathon culture anymore,” one employee posted in a forum open to the entire staff.

item.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and...

item.title: Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it
item.description: A new report suggests the unit, which employs 6,500 people, is on the verge of revolt.

item.title: ‘Tell Him He’s a Piece of Shit’: Meta’s New AI Unit Is a Total Mess
item.description: Executives and employees alike are struggling with Meta’s chaotic AI strategy, according to sources and internal discussions reviewed by WIRED.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid-Inspired Fluid Pump
item.description: This fluid pump was inspired by the way squids propel themselves through the water. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t...

item.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
item.description: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.

item.title: Nothing CEO says phone prices are going to keep going up
item.description: If you're thinking about upgrading your phone, "the best time was yesterday," according to Nothing CEO and co-founder Carl Pei, echoing a message we heard during MWC. As Android Authority reports,...

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
item.description: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware...

item.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
item.description: Vulnerability in the Oracle-owned PeopleSoft software is about as critical as they come.

item.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
item.description: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting...

item.title: The world’s first trillionaire is a killer
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO will probably make him the richest person to ever walk the planet. And while his mountain of horrible personal conduct could fill multiple books, one fact in particular...

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
item.description: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. Sygnia, which tracks the group...

item.title: Mistral is rumored to be raising €3B at €20B valuation
item.description: The funding round would value the company at around €20 billion (about $23.15 billion), nearly double its Series C valuation of €11.7 billion.

item.title: Siri is good now??
item.description: You'd be forgiven for thinking this day would never come. Siri has spent a decade and half somewhere between "sort of useful at a few things" and "utterly disastrous, why did I even try, can it...
